     4→GP <- GlobalPatterns

     7→    data_fungi,
     8→    data_fungi@sam_data$Tree_name %in% c("A10-005", "AD30-abm-X")
     9→  )
    10→GP_archae <-
    11→  subset_taxa(GlobalPatterns, GlobalPatterns@tax_table[, 1] == "Archaea")
    12→GP_archae <- clean_pq(rarefy_even_depth(subset_samples_pq(GP_archae, sample_sums(GP_archae) > 1000)))
    13→data_basidio <- subset_taxa(data_fungi, Phylum == "Basidiomycota")
    14→
    15→test_that("hill_pq works with data_fungi dataset", {
    16→  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height")))
    17→  skip_on_cran()
    18→  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height", add_points = TRUE)))
    19→  expect_silent(suppressMessages(hill_pq(
    20→    clean_pq(subset_samples_pq(

    22→    )), "Height",
    23→    letters = TRUE
    24→  )))
    25→  expect_silent(suppressMessages(
    26→    hill_pq(

    28→      "Height",
    29→      add_points = TRUE,
    30→      color_fac = "Time"
    31→    )
    32→  ))
    33→  expect_silent(

    37→        "Height",
    38→        add_points = TRUE,
    39→        color_fac = "Time",
    40→        one_plot = TRUE,
    41→        correction_for_sample_size = FALSE
    42→      )
    43→    )
    44→  )
    45→  expect_silent(suppressWarnings(suppressMessages(
    46→    hill_pq(

    49→      )),

    59→  expect_s3_class(hill_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height", add_points = TRUE)[[1]], "ggplot")
    60→})

nsamples(new_physeq) > 0) { 63→ message( 64→ paste0( 65→ nsamples(physeq) - nsamples(new_physeq), 66→ " were discarded due to NA in variables present in formula." 67→ ) 68→ ) 69→ } 70→ physeq <- new_physeq 71→ } 72→ 73→ res_graph_test <- phyloseqGraphTest::graph_perm_test(physeq, 74→ sampletype = fact, 75→ nperm = nperm, 76→ ... 77→ ) 78→ if (return_plot) { 79→ if (res_graph_test$type == "mst") { 80→ layout <- igraph::layout_(res_graph_test$net, igraph::with_kk()) 81→ } else { 82→ layout <- igraph::layout_(res_graph_test$net, igraph::with_fr()) 83→ } 84→ p <- ggplot(res_graph_test$net, 85→ aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend), 86→ layout = layout 87→ ) + 88→ ggnetwork::geom_edges(aes(linetype = edgetype)) + 89→ ggnetwork::geom_nodes(aes(color = sampletype)) + 90→ scale_linetype_manual(values = c(3, 1)) + 91→ ggnetwork::theme_blank() + 92→ labs( 93→ title = title, 94→ subtitle = paste( 95→ "pvalue = ", 96→ res_graph_test$pval, 97→ "(", 98→ length(res_graph_test$perm), 99→ " permutations)" 100→ ) 101→ ) 102→ return(p) 103→ } else { 104→ return(res_graph_test) 105→ } 106→} 107→################################################################################ 108→ 109→################################################################################ 110→#' @title Permanova on a phyloseq object 111→#' @description 112→#' 113→#' 114→#' 115→#' 116→#' A wrapper for the [vegan::adonis2()] function in the case of `physeq` object. 117→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 118→#' @param formula (required) the right part of a formula for [vegan::adonis2()]. 119→#' Variables must be present in the `physeq@sam_data` slot.

700→#' @author Adrien Taudière
   701→#' @details
   702→#' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others.
   703→#'   Please make a reference to `indicspecies::multipatt()` if you
   704→#'   use this function.
   705→
   706→multipatt_pq <- function(physeq,
   707→                         fact,
   708→                         p_adjust_method = "BH",
   709→                         pval = 0.05,
   710→                         control = permute::how(nperm = 999),
   711→                         ...) {
   712→  physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq)
   713→
   714→  res <-
   715→    indicspecies::multipatt(as.matrix(physeq@otu_table),
   716→      physeq@sam_data[[fact]],
   717→      control = control,
   718→      ...
   719→    )
   720→
   721→  res_df <- res$sign
   722→  res_df$p.adj <- p.adjust(res_df$p.value, method = p_adjust_method)
   723→  res_df$taxon_names <- rownames(res_df)
   724→  res_df_signif <-
   725→    res_df %>%
   726→    filter(p.adj < pval) %>%
   727→    tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("s."))
   728→
   729→  p <- ggplot(
   730→    res_df_signif,
   731→    aes(
   732→      x = taxon_names,
   733→      y = name,
   734→      size = 2 * value,
   735→      color = stat
   736→    )
   737→  ) +
   738→    geom_point() +
   739→    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(
   740→      angle = 90,
   741→      vjust = 0.5,
   742→      hjust = 1
   743→    ))
   744→  return(p)
   745→}
   746→################################################################################
   747→
   748→################################################################################
   749→#' Run ANCOMBC2 on phyloseq object

content.content: 60→#' 61→#' 62→#' 63→#' Note that as most bioinformatic pipeline discard singleton, accumulation curves from metabarcoding 64→#' cannot be interpreted in the same way as with conventional biodiversity sampling techniques. 65→#' 66→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 67→#' @param fact (required) Name of the factor in `physeq@sam_data` used to plot 68→#' different lines 69→#' @param add_nb_seq (default: TRUE, logical) 70→#' Either plot accumulation curves using sequences or using samples 71→#' @param step (Integer) distance among points calculated to plot lines. A 72→#' low value give better plot but is more time consuming. 73→#' Only used if `add_nb_seq` = TRUE. 74→#' @param by.fact (default: FALSE, logical) 75→#' First merge the OTU table by factor to plot only one line by factor 76→#' @param ci_col Color vector for confidence interval. 77→#' Only use if `add_nb_seq` = FALSE. 78→#' If `add_nb_seq` = TRUE, you can use ggplot to modify the plot. 79→#' @param col Color vector for lines. Only use if `add_nb_seq` = FALSE. 80→#' If `add_nb_seq` = TRUE, you can use ggplot to modify the plot. 81→#' @param lwd (default: 3) thickness for lines. Only use if `add_nb_seq` = FALSE. 82→#' @param leg (default: TRUE, logical) Plot legend or not. Only use if `add_nb_seq` = FALSE. 83→#' @param print_sam_names (default: FALSE, logical) Print samples names or not? 84→#' Only use if `add_nb_seq` = TRUE. 85→#' @param ci (default: 2, integer) Confidence interval value used to multiply the 86→#' standard error to plot confidence interval 87→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to \code{\link[ggplot2]{ggplot}} 88→#' if `add_nb_seq` = TRUE or to \code{\link{plot}} if `add_nb_seq` = FALSE 89→#' 90→#' @examples 91→#' \donttest{ 92→#' data("GlobalPatterns", package = "phyloseq") 93→#' GP <- subset_taxa(GlobalPatterns, GlobalPatterns@tax_table[, 1] == "Archaea") 94→#' GP <- rarefy_even_depth(subset_samples_pq(GP, sample_sums(GP) > 3000)) 95→#' p <- accu_plot(GP, "SampleType", add_nb_seq = TRUE, by.fact = TRUE, step = 10) 96→#' p <- accu_plot(GP, "SampleType", add_nb_seq = TRUE, step = 10) 97→#' 98→#' p + theme(legend.position = "none") 99→#' 100→#' p + xlim(c(0, 400)) 101→#' } 102→#' @return A \code{\link[ggplot2]{ggplot}}2 plot representing the richness 103→#' accumulation plot if add_nb_seq = TRUE, else, if add_nb_seq = FALSE 104→#' return a base plot. 105→#' 106→#' @export 107→#' @author Adrien Taudière 108→#' @seealso \code{\link[vegan]{specaccum}} [accu_samp_threshold()] 109→accu_plot <- 110→ function( 111→ physeq, 112→ fact = NULL, 113→ add_nb_seq = TRUE, 114→ step = NULL, 115→ by.fact = FALSE, 116→ ci_col = NULL, 117→ col = NULL, 118→ lwd = 3, 119→ leg = TRUE, 120→ print_sam_names = FALSE, 121→ ci = 2, 122→ ... 123→ ) { 124→ if (!inherits(physeq, "phyloseq")) { 125→ stop("physeq must be a phyloseq object") 126→ } 127→ 128→ if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { 129→ physeq@otu_table <- 130→ otu_table(t(physeq@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = TRUE) 131→ } 132→ 133→ if (!add_nb_seq) { 134→ factor_interm <- 135→ eval(parse(text = paste("physeq@sam_data$", fact, sep = ""))) 136→ factor_interm <- as.factor(factor_interm) 137→ 138→ physeq_accu <- as.matrix(t(physeq@otu_table)) 139→ physeq_accu[physeq_accu > 0] <- 1

1039→#' @author Adrien Taudière
  1040→#' @seealso \code{\link[venneuler]{venneuler}}
  1041→
  1042→venn_pq <-
  1043→  function(physeq, fact, min_nb_seq = 0, print_values = TRUE) {
  1044→    if (!inherits(physeq, "phyloseq")) {
  1045→      stop("physeq must be an object of class 'phyloseq'")
  1046→    }
  1047→
  1048→    moda <-
  1049→      as.factor(unlist(unclass(physeq@sam_data[, fact])[fact]))
  1050→    if (length(moda) != dim(physeq@otu_table)[1]) {
  1051→      data_venn <-
  1052→        t(apply(physeq@otu_table, 1, function(x) {
  1053→          by(x, moda, max)
  1054→        }))
  1055→    } else if (length(moda) != dim(physeq@otu_table)[2]) {
  1056→      data_venn <-
  1057→        t(apply(t(physeq@otu_table), 1, function(x) {
  1058→          by(x, moda, max)
  1059→        }))
  1060→    } else {
  1061→      stop("The factor length and the number of samples must be identical")
  1062→    }
  1063→    combinations <- data_venn > min_nb_seq
  1064→
  1065→    e <- new.env(TRUE, emptyenv())
  1066→    cn <- colnames(combinations)
  1067→    for (i in seq.int(dim(combinations)[1])) {
  1068→      if (any(combinations[i, ])) {
  1069→        ec <- paste(cn[combinations[i, ]], collapse = "&")
  1070→        e[[ec]] <- if (is.null(e[[ec]])) {
  1071→          1L
  1072→        } else {
  1073→          (e[[ec]] + 1L)
  1074→        }
  1075→      }
  1076→    }
  1077→
  1078→    en <- ls(e, all.names = TRUE)
  1079→    weights <- as.numeric(unlist(lapply(en, get, e)))
  1080→    combinations <- as.character(en)
  1081→
  1082→    table_value <-
  1083→      data.frame(
  1084→        combinations = as.character(combinations),
  1085→        weights = as.double(weights),
  1086→        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  1087→      )
  1088→
  1089→    venn <- venneuler::venneuler(data_venn > min_nb_seq)
  1090→    venn_res <-
  1091→      data.frame(
  1092→        x = venn$centers[, 1],
  1093→        y = venn$centers[, 2],
  1094→        radius = venn$diameters / 2
  1095→      )
  1096→
  1097→    nmod <- nrow(venn_res)
  1098→    x1 <- vector("list", nmod)

Internally used in [accu_plot_balanced_modality()]. 3851→#' 3852→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 3853→#' @param fact (required) The variable to rarefy. Must be present in 3854→#' the `sam_data` slot of the physeq object. 3855→#' @param rngseed (Optional). A single integer value passed to set.seed, 3856→#' which is used to fix a seed for reproducibly random number generation 3857→#' (in this case, reproducibly random subsampling). If set to FALSE, then no 3858→#' iddling with the RNG seed is performed, and it is up to the user to 3859→#' appropriately call 3860→#' @param verbose (logical). If TRUE, print additional information. 3861→#' @export 3862→#' @author Adrien Taudière 3863→#' @return A new \code{\link[phyloseq]{phyloseq-class}} object. 3864→#' @seealso [accu_plot_balanced_modality()] 3865→#' @examples 3866→#' table(data_fungi_mini@sam_data$Height) 3867→#' data_fungi_mini2 <- rarefy_sample_count_by_modality(data_fungi_mini, "Height") 3868→#' table(data_fungi_mini2@sam_data$Height) 3869→#' if (requireNamespace("patchwork")) { 3870→#' ggvenn_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height") + ggvenn_pq(data_fungi_mini2, "Height") 3871→#' } 3872→rarefy_sample_count_by_modality <- 3873→ function(physeq, 3874→ fact, 3875→ rngseed = FALSE, 3876→ verbose = TRUE) { 3877→ if (as(rngseed, "logical")) { 3878→ set.seed(rngseed) 3879→ if (verbose) { 3880→ message( 3881→ "`set.seed(", 3882→ rngseed, 3883→ ")` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling." 3884→ ) 3885→ message("Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.") 3886→ message("Try `set.seed(", 3887→ rngseed, 3888→ "); .Random.seed` for the full vector", 3889→ sep = "" 3890→ ) 3891→ } 3892→ } else if (verbose) { 3893→ message( 3894→ "You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded\n", 3895→ " the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n", 3896→ "See `?set.seed`\n" 3897→ ) 3898→ message("...") 3899→ } 3900→ mod <- as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 3901→ n_mod <- table(mod) 3902→ samp_to_keep <- NULL 3903→ for (modality in levels(mod)) { 3904→ vec_samp_mod <- c(as.numeric(grep(modality, mod))) 3905→ 3906→ # To bypass the pb of vector of length 1 3907→ # We build a vector of two equal values and we will take only one 3908→ # It is cause by range base behavior: 3909→ # 'If x has length 1, is numeric (in the sense of is.numeric) and x >= 1, sampling via sample takes place from 1:x.' 3910→ if (length(vec_samp_mod) == 1) { 3911→ vec_samp_mod <- c(vec_samp_mod, vec_samp_mod) 3912→ } 3913→ samp_to_keep <- 3914→ c( 3915→ samp_to_keep, 3916→ sample(vec_samp_mod, size = min(n_mod), replace = FALSE) 3917→ ) 3918→ } 3919→ new_physeq <-

528→#'
   529→#' Graphical representation of distribution of taxa across a factor.
   530→#'
   531→#' @inheritParams clean_pq
   532→#' @param fact (required) Name of the factor to cluster samples by modalities.
   533→#'        Need to be in \code{physeq@sam_data}.
   534→#' @param taxa (default: 'Order') Name of the taxonomic rank of interest
   535→#' @param nproc (default 1)
   536→#'   Set to number of cpus/processors to use for parallelization
   537→#' @param add_nb_seq (logical, default TRUE) Represent the number of sequences or the
   538→#'    number of OTUs (add_nb_seq = FALSE)
   539→#' @param rarefy (logical) Does each samples modalities need to be rarefy in
   540→#'               order to compare them with the same amount of sequences?
   541→#' @param min_prop_tax (default: 0.01) The minimum proportion for taxa to be
   542→#'                     plotted
   543→#' @param min_prop_mod (default: 0.1) The minimum proportion for modalities
   544→#'                     to be plotted
   545→#' @param gap_degree Gap between two neighbour sectors.
   546→#'                    It can be a single value or a vector. If it is a vector,
   547→#'                     the first value corresponds to the gap after the first
   548→#'                     sector.
   549→#' @param start_degree The starting degree from which the circle begins to
   550→#'   draw. Note this degree is measured in the standard polar coordinate
   551→#'   which means it is always reverse-clockwise.
   552→#' @param row_col Color vector for row
   553→#' @param grid_col Grid colors which correspond to sectors. The length of the
   554→#'    vector should be either 1 or the number of sectors.
   555→#'    It's preferred that grid_col is a named vector of which names
   556→#'    correspond to sectors. If it is not a named vector, the
   557→#'    order of grid_col corresponds to order of sectors.

```r
                      verbose = TRUE,
                      fact = NULL,
                      ...)
```

---

## 2. Test file directory structure

Located at `/home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/MiscMetabar/tests/testthat/`. There are **35 test files** plus one subdirectory `inst/extdata/` (containing fasta/fastq fixtures) and `_snaps/`. No `helper-*.R` files exist.

Key files relevant to `fact`-using functions:
- `test_figures_alpha_div.R` - tests `hill_pq`, `iNEXT_pq`, `accu_plot`
- `test_figures_beta_div.R` - tests `circle_pq`, `graph_test_pq`, `ggvenn_pq`, `venn_pq`, `upset_pq`
- `test_figures_taxo.R` - tests `tax_bar_pq`, `reorder_colors`, `treemap_pq`
- `test_beta_div.R` - tests `adonis_rarperm_pq`, `var_par_pq`
- `test_adonis.R` - tests `adonis_pq`
- `test_ancombc.R` - tests `ancombc_pq`
- `test_plot_funcs.R` - tests `accu_plot_balanced_modality`, `plot_complexity_pq`

---

## 3. Test fixtures and phyloseq object creation patterns

**No `helper-*.R` files exist.** Phyloseq objects are set up at the top of each test file as top-level code (run before `test_that()` calls). The main pattern is:

```r
# Most common: load package datasets
data(data_fungi)        # from MiscMetabar
data(data_fungi_mini)   # from MiscMetabar (smaller version)
data("GlobalPatterns", package = "phyloseq")
data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq")

# Then subset as needed
GP <- GlobalPatterns
data_fungi_2trees <- subset_samples(data_fungi, data_fungi@sam_data$Tree_name %in% c("A10-005", "AD30-abm-X"))
data_subset <- subset_samples(data_fungi, Height %in% c("Low", "High")) |>
  subset_samples(!is.na(Time) & !is.na(Height))
```

**Single-sample phyloseq objects** are created inline in tests, not as shared fixtures. The one concrete example is in `test_figures_taxo.R` lines 440-451:

```r
# Single-sample phyloseq (1 sample, 3 taxa)
otu <- matrix(c(100, 100, 1), nrow = 3, ncol = 1)
rownames(otu) <- paste0("ASV", 1:3)
colnames(otu) <- "S1"
tax <- matrix(c("A", "A", "B"), ncol = 1)
colnames(tax) <- "Genus"
rownames(tax) <- rownames(otu)
sam <- data.frame(Type = "T0", row.names = "S1")
ps <- phyloseq::phyloseq(
  phyloseq::otu_table(otu, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
  phyloseq::tax_table(tax),
  phyloseq::sample_data(sam)
)
```

Another multi-sample-but-reduced approach in `test_dada_phyloseq.R` (lines 154-158):

```r
physeq_no_refseq <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(data_fungi_mini@otu_table),
  sample_data(data_fungi_mini@sam_data),
  tax_table(data_fungi_mini@tax_table)
)
```

Effectively, there are **no shared fixture files** - all objects are local to each test file.

---

## 4. Summary of the `fact` parameter pattern

Across all functions, `fact` is always:
- A **character string** naming a column in `physeq@sam_data`
- Accessed internally via `physeq@sam_data[[fact]]` or `physeq@sam_data[, fact]`
- Often coerced to factor: `as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])`
- Sometimes NA-filtered before use: `subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]))`
- Required (no default) in: `graph_test_pq`, `multipatt_pq`, `ancombc_pq`, `distri_1_taxa`, `are_modality_even_depth`, `rarefy_sample_count_by_modality`, `ggbetween_pq`, `venn_pq`, `upset_pq`, `ridges_pq`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq`
- Optional with `NULL` default in: `accu_plot`, `circle_pq`, `sankey_pq`, `ggvenn_pq`, `hill_pq`, `biplot_pq`, `plot_tax_pq`, `multitax_bar_pq`, `ggaluv_pq`
- Special cases: `tax_bar_pq` defaults to `"Sample"`; `plot_tsne_pq` defaults to `NA`

---

## 2. Test file directory structure

Located at `/home/adrien/Nextcloud/IdEst/Projets/MiscMetabar/tests/testthat/`. There are **35 test files** plus one subdirectory `inst/extdata/` (containing fasta/fastq fixtures) and `_snaps/`. No `helper-*.R` files exist.

Key files relevant to `fact`-using functions:
- `test_figures_alpha_div.R` - tests `hill_pq`, `iNEXT_pq`, `accu_plot`
- `test_figures_beta_div.R` - tests `circle_pq`, `graph_test_pq`, `ggvenn_pq`, `venn_pq`, `upset_pq`
- `test_figures_taxo.R` - tests `tax_bar_pq`, `reorder_colors`, `treemap_pq`
- `test_beta_div.R` - tests `adonis_rarperm_pq`, `var_par_pq`
- `test_adonis.R` - tests `adonis_pq`
- `test_ancombc.R` - tests `ancombc_pq`
- `test_plot_funcs.R` - tests `accu_plot_balanced_modality`, `plot_complexity_pq`

---

## 3. Test fixtures and phyloseq object creation patterns

**No `helper-*.R` files exist.** Phyloseq objects are set up at the top of each test file as top-level code (run before `test_that()` calls). The main pattern is:

```r
# Most common: load package datasets
data(data_fungi)        # from MiscMetabar
data(data_fungi_mini)   # from MiscMetabar (smaller version)
data("GlobalPatterns", package = "phyloseq")
data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq")

# Then subset as needed
GP <- GlobalPatterns
data_fungi_2trees <- subset_samples(data_fungi, data_fungi@sam_data$Tree_name %in% c("A10-005", "AD30-abm-X"))
data_subset <- subset_samples(data_fungi, Height %in% c("Low", "High")) |>
  subset_samples(!is.na(Time) & !is.na(Height))
```

**Single-sample phyloseq objects** are created inline in tests, not as shared fixtures. The one concrete example is in `test_figures_taxo.R` lines 440-451:

```r
# Single-sample phyloseq (1 sample, 3 taxa)
otu <- matrix(c(100, 100, 1), nrow = 3, ncol = 1)
rownames(otu) <- paste0("ASV", 1:3)
colnames(otu) <- "S1"
tax <- matrix(c("A", "A", "B"), ncol = 1)
colnames(tax) <- "Genus"
rownames(tax) <- rownames(otu)
sam <- data.frame(Type = "T0", row.names = "S1")
ps <- phyloseq::phyloseq(
  phyloseq::otu_table(otu, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
  phyloseq::tax_table(tax),
  phyloseq::sample_data(sam)
)
```

Another multi-sample-but-reduced approach in `test_dada_phyloseq.R` (lines 154-158):

```r
physeq_no_refseq <- phyloseq(
  otu_table(data_fungi_mini@otu_table),
  sample_data(data_fungi_mini@sam_data),
  tax_table(data_fungi_mini@tax_table)
)
```

Effectively, there are **no shared fixture files** - all objects are local to each test file.

---

## 4. Summary of the `fact` parameter pattern

Across all functions, `fact` is always:
- A **character string** naming a column in `physeq@sam_data`
- Accessed internally via `physeq@sam_data[[fact]]` or `physeq@sam_data[, fact]`
- Often coerced to factor: `as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])`
- Sometimes NA-filtered before use: `subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]))`
- Required (no default) in: `graph_test_pq`, `multipatt_pq`, `ancombc_pq`, `distri_1_taxa`, `are_modality_even_depth`, `rarefy_sample_count_by_modality`, `ggbetween_pq`, `venn_pq`, `upset_pq`, `ridges_pq`, `hill_test_rarperm_pq`
- Optional with `NULL` default in: `accu_plot`, `circle_pq`, `sankey_pq`, `ggvenn_pq`, `hill_pq`, `biplot_pq`, `plot_tax_pq`, `multitax_bar_pq`, `ggaluv_pq`
- Special cases: `tax_bar_pq` defaults to `"Sample"`; `plot_tsne_pq` defaults to `NA`
Now let me look at how some of these functions handle (or fail to handle) single-sample phyloseq objects with the `fact` parameter.

test_that("treemap_pq log10 transform gives nonzero area for count 1", {
  if (requireNamespace("treemapify")) {
    skip_on_cran()
    otu <- matrix(c(100, 100, 1), nrow = 3, ncol = 1)
    rownames(otu) <- paste0("ASV", 1:3)
    colnames(otu) <- "S1"
    tax <- matrix(c("A", "A", "B"), ncol = 1)
    colnames(tax) <- "Genus"
    rownames(tax) <- rownames(otu)
    sam <- data.frame(Type = "T0", row.names = "S1")
    ps <- phyloseq::phyloseq(
      phyloseq::otu_table(otu, taxa_are_rows = TRUE),
      phyloseq::tax_table(tax),
      phyloseq::sample_data(sam)
    )
    p <- treemap_pq(ps, "Genus", "Genus", nb_seq = TRUE)
    df <- ggplot2::ggplot_build(p)$data[[1]]
    expect_equal(nrow(df), 2)
    expect_true(all(df$xmax - df$xmin > 0))
    expect_true(all(df$ymax - df$ymin > 0))
  }
})

In this R package (MiscMetabar), I need to understand how functions handle the `fact` parameter when there's only one sample in the phyloseq object.

Search for patterns like:
1. Any validation/checks on `fact` - search for patterns like "nsamples", "length(levels", "nlevels" near `fact` usage in R/ files

For each function, note:
- Whether fact is required or optional
- Any validation on number of samples or factor levels
- What would likely happen with 1 sample (error, warning, empty plot, etc.)

Be very thorough in reading the function bodies.

content.content: 109→accu_plot <- 110→ function( 111→ physeq, 112→ fact = NULL, 113→ add_nb_seq = TRUE, 114→ step = NULL, 115→ by.fact = FALSE, 116→ ci_col = NULL, 117→ col = NULL, 118→ lwd = 3, 119→ leg = TRUE, 120→ print_sam_names = FALSE, 121→ ci = 2, 122→ ... 123→ ) { 124→ if (!inherits(physeq, "phyloseq")) { 125→ stop("physeq must be a phyloseq object") 126→ } 127→ 128→ if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq)) { 129→ physeq@otu_table <- 130→ otu_table(t(physeq@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = TRUE) 131→ } 132→ 133→ if (!add_nb_seq) { 134→ factor_interm <- 135→ eval(parse(text = paste("physeq@sam_data$", fact, sep = ""))) 136→ factor_interm <- as.factor(factor_interm) 137→ 138→ physeq_accu <- as.matrix(t(physeq@otu_table)) 139→ physeq_accu[physeq_accu > 0] <- 1 140→ accu_all <- vegan::specaccum(physeq_accu) 141→ 142→ accu <- vector("list", nlevels(factor_interm)) 143→ for (i in seq_along(levels(factor_interm))) { 144→ accu[[i]] <- 145→ vegan::specaccum(physeq_accu[ 146→ factor_interm == levels(factor_interm)[i], 147→ ]) 148→ } 149→ 150→ if (is.null(col)) { 151→ col <- funky_color(nlevels(factor_interm) + 1) 152→ } 153→ if (is.null(ci_col)) { 154→ transp <- function(col, alpha = 0.5) { 155→ res <- 156→ apply(grDevices::col2rgb(col), 2, function(c) { 157→ grDevices::rgb(c[1] / 255, c[2] / 255, c[3] / 255, alpha) 158→ }) 159→ return(res) 160→ } 161→ ci_col <- 162→ transp(funky_color(nlevels(factor_interm) + 1), 0.3) 163→ } 164→ 165→ plot( 166→ accu_all, 167→ # ci_type = "poly", 168→ # ci_col = ci_col[1], 169→ col = col[1], 170→ lwd = lwd, 171→ # ci_lty = 0, 172→ xlab = "Sample", 173→ ... 174→ ) 175→ 176→ for (i in seq_along(levels(factor_interm))) { 177→ graphics::lines(accu[[i]], col = col[i + 1], lwd = lwd) 178→ } 179→ if (leg) { 180→ graphics::legend( 181→ "bottomright", 182→ c("all", levels(factor_interm)), 183→ col = col, 184→ lty = 1, 185→ lwd = 3 186→ ) 187→ } 188→ } 189→ 190→ if (add_nb_seq) { 191→ fact_interm <- 192→ as.factor(unlist(unclass(physeq@sam_data[, fact])[fact])) 193→ 194→ if (by.fact) { 195→ x <- apply(physeq@otu_table, 1, function(x) { 196→ tapply(x, fact_interm, sum) 197→ }) 198→ } else { 199→ x <- t(physeq@otu_table) 200→ } 201→ 202→ tot <- rowSums(x) 203→ nr <- nrow(x) 204→ 205→ if (is.null(step)) { 206→ step <- round(max(tot) / 30, 0) 207→ } 208→ 209→ n_max <- seq(1, max(tot), by = step) 210→ out <- lapply(seq_len(nr), function(i) { 211→ n <- seq(1, tot[i], by = step) 212→ if (n[length(n)] != tot[i]) { 213→ n <- c(n, tot[i]) 214→ } 215→ res_interm <- 216→ vegan::rarefy(as.matrix(unclass(x[i, ])), n, se = TRUE) 217→ res <- 218→ cbind(as.matrix(res_interm)[1, ], as.matrix(res_interm)[2, ]) 219→ return(res) 220→ }) 221→ 222→ names(out) <- names(tot) 223→ 224→ df <- plyr::ldply(out, data.frame) 225→ 226→ cond <- vector(mode = "logical") 227→ for (i in seq_along(levels(as.factor(df$.id)))) { 228→ cond <- c(cond, 1:table(df$.id)[i]) 229→ } 230→ 231→ df$x <- n_max[cond] 232→ 233→ if (by.fact) { 234→ df$fact <- df$.id 235→ } else { 236→ df$fact <- 237→ as.factor(unlist(unclass(physeq@sam_data[ 238→ match(df$.id, sample_names(physeq)), 239→ fact 240→ ])[fact])) 241→ } 242→ 243→ df$ymin <- df$X1 - df$X2 * ci 244→ df$ymin[is.na(df$ymin)] <- df$X1[is.na(df$ymin)] 245→ df$ymax <- df$X1 + df$X2 * ci 246→ df$ymax[is.na(df$ymax)] <- df$X1[is.na(df$ymax)] 247→ dff <- data.frame(matrix(nrow = length(tot))) 248→ dff$xlab <- tapply(df$x, df$.id, max) 249→ dff$xlab <- dff$xlab + max(dff$xlab, na.rm = TRUE) / 20 250→ dff$ylab <- tapply(df$X1, df$.id, max) 251→ dff$.id <- names(dff$ylab) 252→ p <- 253→ ggplot( 254→ data = df, 255→ aes( 256→ x = x, 257→ y = X1, 258→ group = .id, 259→ col = fact 260→ ) 261→ ) + 262→ geom_ribbon( 263→ aes( 264→ ymin = ymin, 265→ ymax = ymax, 266→ col = NULL, 267→ fill = fact 268→ ), 269→ alpha = 0.2 270→ ) + 271→ geom_line() + 272→ xlab("Number of sequences") + 273→ ylab("Number of OTUs (with standard error)") 274→ 275→ if (print_sam_names) { 276→ p + 277→ geom_text( 278→ data = dff, 279→ aes( 280→ x = xlab, 281→ y = ylab, 282→ label = .id, 283→ col = NULL 284→ ) 285→ ) 286→ } else { 287→ p 288→ } 289→ return(p) 290→ } 291→ } 292→################################################################################ 293→ 294→################################################################################ 295→#' Plot accumulation curves with balanced modality and depth rarefaction 296→#' @description 297→#' 298→#' 299→#' 300→#' 301→#' This function (i) rarefy (equalize) the number of samples per modality of a 302→#' factor and (ii) rarefy the number of sequences per sample (depth). The 303→#' seed is set to 1:nperm. Thus, with exacly the same parameter, including 304→#' nperm values, results must be identical. 305→#' 306→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 307→#' @param fact (required) The variable to rarefy. Must be present in 308→#' the `sam_data` slot of the physeq object. 309→#' @param nperm (int) The number of permutations to perform. 310→#' @param step (int) distance among points calculated to plot lines. 311→#' A low value give better plot but is more time consuming. 312→#' @param by.fact (logical, default TRUE) 313→#' First merge the OTU table by factor to plot only one line by factor 314→#' @param progress_bar (logical, default TRUE) Do we print progress during 315→#' the calculation? 316→#' @param quantile_prob (float, `[0:1]`) the value to compute the quantile. 317→#' Minimum quantile is compute using 1-quantile_prob. 318→#' @param rarefy_by_sample_before_merging (logical, default TRUE): 319→#' rarefy_by_sample_before_merging = FALSE is buggy for the moment.Please 320→#' only use rarefy_by_sample_before_merging = TRUE 321→#' @param sample.size (int) A single integer value equal to the number of 322→#' reads being simulated, also known as the depth. See 323→#' [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()]. 324→#' @param verbose (logical). If TRUE, print additional information. 325→#' @param ... Other params for be passed on to [accu_plot()] function 326→#' 327→#' @export 328→#' @author Adrien Taudière 329→#' @seealso [accu_plot()], [rarefy_sample_count_by_modality()], [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()] 330→#' 331→#' @return A ggplot2 plot representing the richness accumulation plot 332→#' @examples 333→#' \donttest{ 334→#' data_fungi_woNA4Time <- 335→#' subset_samples(data_fungi, !is.na(Time)) 336→#' data_fungi_woNA4Time@sam_data$Time <- paste0("time-", data_fungi_woNA4Time@sam_data$Time) 337→#' accu_plot_balanced_modality(data_fungi_woNA4Time, "Time", nperm = 3) 338→#'

content.content: 583→circle_pq <- 584→ function( 585→ physeq = NULL, 586→ fact = NULL, 587→ taxa = "Order", 588→ nproc = 1, 589→ add_nb_seq = TRUE, 590→ rarefy = FALSE, 591→ min_prop_tax = 0.01, 592→ min_prop_mod = 0.1, 593→ gap_degree = NULL, 594→ start_degree = NULL, 595→ row_col = NULL, 596→ grid_col = NULL, 597→ log10trans = FALSE, 598→ ... 599→ ) { 600→ if (!inherits(physeq, "phyloseq")) { 601→ stop("physeq must be an object of class 'phyloseq'") 602→ } 603→ 604→ if (physeq@otu_table@taxa_are_rows) { 605→ otu_tab <- physeq@otu_table 606→ } else { 607→ otu_tab <- t(physeq@otu_table) 608→ } 609→ 610→ if (!add_nb_seq) { 611→ otu_tab[otu_tab > 0] <- 1 612→ } 613→ 614→ taxcol <- match(taxa, colnames(physeq@tax_table)) 615→ if (is.na(taxcol)) { 616→ stop("The taxa argument do not match any taxa rank in physeq@tax_table") 617→ } 618→ 619→ taxsamp <- match(fact, colnames(physeq@sam_data)) 620→ if (is.na(taxsamp)) { 621→ stop( 622→ "The samples argument do not match any sample attributes 623→ in physeq@sam_data" 624→ ) 625→ } 626→ 627→ otu_table_tax <- 628→ pbapply::pbapply( 629→ otu_tab, 630→ 2, 631→ function(x) { 632→ pbapply::pbtapply( 633→ x, 634→ physeq@tax_table[, taxcol], 635→ function(xx) { 636→ sum(xx, na.rm = TRUE) 637→ } 638→ ) 639→ }, 640→ cl = nproc 641→ ) 642→ otu_table_ech <- 643→ pbapply::pbapply( 644→ otu_table_tax, 645→ 1, 646→ function(x) { 647→ pbapply::pbtapply( 648→ x, 649→ physeq@sam_data[, taxsamp], 650→ function(xx) { 651→ sum(xx, na.rm = TRUE) 652→ } 653→ ) 654→ }, 655→ cl = nproc 656→ ) 657→ if (rarefy) { 658→ otu_table_ech_interm <- 659→ vegan::rrarefy(otu_table_ech, min(rowSums(otu_table_ech))) 660→ message( 661→ paste( 662→ "Rarefaction by modalities deletes ", 663→ sum(otu_table_ech) - nsamples(new_physeq) > 0) { 63→ message( 64→ paste0( 65→ nsamples(physeq) - nsamples(new_physeq), 66→ " were discarded due to NA in variables present in formula." 67→ ) 68→ ) 69→ } 70→ physeq <- new_physeq 71→ } 72→ 73→ res_graph_test <- phyloseqGraphTest::graph_perm_test(physeq, 74→ sampletype = fact, 75→ nperm = nperm, 76→ ... 77→ ) 78→ if (return_plot) { 79→ if (res_graph_test$type == "mst") { 80→ layout <- igraph::layout_(res_graph_test$net, igraph::with_kk()) 81→ } else { 82→ layout <- igraph::layout_(res_graph_test$net, igraph::with_fr()) 83→ } 84→ p <- ggplot(res_graph_test$net, 85→ aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend), 86→ layout = layout 87→ ) + 88→ ggnetwork::geom_edges(aes(linetype = edgetype)) + 89→ ggnetwork::geom_nodes(aes(color = sampletype)) + 90→ scale_linetype_manual(values = c(3, 1)) + 91→ ggnetwork::theme_blank() + 92→ labs( 93→ title = title, 94→ subtitle = paste( 95→ "pvalue = ", 96→ res_graph_test$pval, 97→ "(", 98→ length(res_graph_test$perm), 99→ " permutations)" 100→ ) 101→ ) 102→ return(p) 103→ } else { 104→ return(res_graph_test) 105→ } 106→} 107→################################################################################ 108→ 109→################################################################################ 110→#' @title Permanova on a phyloseq object 111→#' @description 112→#' 113→#' 114→#' 115→#' 116→#' A wrapper for the [vegan::adonis2()] function in the case of `physeq` object. 117→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 118→#' @param formula (required) the right part of a formula for [vegan::adonis2()]. 119→#' Variables must be present in the `physeq@sam_data` slot. 120→#' @param dist_method (default "bray") the distance used. See 121→#' [phyloseq::distance()] for all available distances or run 122→#' [phyloseq::distanceMethodList()]. 123→#' For aitchison and robust.aitchison distance, [vegan::vegdist()] 124→#' function is directly used. 125→#' @param by (character, default "terms") by = "terms" will assess significance 126→#' for each term (sequentially from first to last); if by = NULL , 127→#' the p-value is computed for the entire model i.e. the overall significance 128→#' of all terms together is computed, setting by = "margin" will assess the 129→#' marginal effects of the terms (each marginal term analyzed in a model 130→#' with all other variables), by = "onedf" will analyze one-degree-of-freedom 131→#' contrasts sequentially. See `?vegan::adonis2` for more information. 132→#' @param merge_sample_by a vector to determine 133→#' which samples to merge using the [merge_samples2()] 134→#' function. Need to be in `physeq@sam_data` 135→#' @param na_remove (logical, default FALSE) If set to TRUE, remove samples with 136→#' NA in the variables set in formula. 137→#' @param correction_for_sample_size (logical, default FALSE) If set to TRUE, 138→#' the sample size (number of sequences by samples) is added to formula in 139→#' the form `y~Library_Size + Biological_Effect` following recommendation of 140→#' [Weiss et al. 2017](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s40168-017-0237-y). 141→#' `correction_for_sample_size` overcome `rarefy_nb_seqs` if both are TRUE. 142→#' @param rarefy_nb_seqs (logical, default FALSE) Rarefy each sample 143→#' (before merging if merge_sample_by is set) using 144→#' `phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()`. 145→#' if `correction_for_sample_size` is TRUE, rarefy_nb_seqs will have no 146→#' effect. 147→#' @param rngseed (Optional). A single integer value passed to 148→#' [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()], which is used to fix a seed for 149→#' reproducibly random number generation (in this case, reproducibly 150→#' random subsampling). If set to FALSE, then no fiddling with the RNG seed 151→#' is performed, and it is up to the user to appropriately call set.seed 152→#' beforehand to achieve reproducible results. Default is FALSE. 153→#' @param verbose (logical, default TRUE) If TRUE, prompt some messages. 154→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [vegan::adonis2()] function. 155→#' @return The function returns an anova.cca result object with a 156→#' new column for partial R^2. See help of [vegan::adonis2()] for 157→#' more information. 158→#' @examples 159→#' data(enterotype) 160→#' \donttest{ 161→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech*Enterotype", na_remove = TRUE) 162→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech*Enterotype", na_remove = TRUE, by = NULL) 163→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech*Enterotype", na_remove = TRUE, by = "onedf") 164→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech*Enterotype", na_remove = TRUE, by = "margin") 165→#' 166→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech", dist_method = "jaccard") 167→#' adonis_pq(enterotype, "SeqTech", dist_method = "robust.aitchison") 168→#' 169→#' adonis_pq(data_fungi, "Time*Height", na_remove = TRUE, correction_for_sample_size = TRUE) 170→#' } 171→#' @export 172→#' @author Adrien Taudière 173→#' @details 174→#' This function is mainly a wrapper of the work of others. 175→#' Please make a reference to `vegan::adonis2()` if you 176→#' use this function. 177→ 178→adonis_pq <- function(physeq, 179→ formula, 180→ dist_method = "bray", 181→ by = "terms", 182→ merge_sample_by = NULL, 183→ na_remove = FALSE, 184→ correction_for_sample_size = FALSE, 185→ rarefy_nb_seqs = FALSE, 186→ rngseed = FALSE, 187→ verbose = TRUE, 188→ ...) { 189→ physeq <- taxa_as_columns(physeq) 190→ 191→ if (dist_method %in% c("aitchison", "robust.aitchison")) { 192→ phy_dist <- 193→ paste0(

Make sure you've set & recorded\n", 1379→ " the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n", 1380→ "See `?set.seed`\n" 1381→ ) 1382→ message("...") 1383→ } 1384→ physeq <- rarefy_even_depth(physeq, rngseed = rngseed) 1385→ physeq <- clean_pq(physeq) 1386→ } 1387→ 1388→ nb_samples <- table(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 1389→ 1390→ if (rarefy_after_merging) { 1391→ physeq <- merge_samples2(physeq, fact) 1392→ if (as(rngseed, "logical")) { 1393→ set.seed(rngseed) 1394→ if (verbose) { 1395→ message( 1396→ "`set.seed(", 1397→ rngseed, 1398→ ")` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling." 1399→ ) 1400→ message("Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.") 1401→ message( 1402→ "Try `set.seed(", 1403→ rngseed, 1404→ "); .Random.seed` for the full vector", 1405→ sep = "" 1406→ ) 1407→ message("...") 1408→ } 1409→ } else if (verbose) { 1410→ message( 1411→ "You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded\n", 1412→ " the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n", 1413→ "See `?set.seed`\n" 1414→ ) 1415→ message("...") 1416→ } 1417→ physeq <- rarefy_even_depth(physeq, rngseed = rngseed) 1418→ physeq <- clean_pq(physeq) 1419→ } 1420→ 1421→ res <- vector("list", nlevels(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) 1422→ names(res) <- levels(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 1423→ nb_seq <- vector(mode = "integer") 1424→ 1425→ for (f in levels(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) { 1426→ newphyseq <- physeq 1427→ new_DF <- newphyseq@sam_data[ 1428→ newphyseq@sam_data[[fact]] == f, 1429→ , 1430→ drop = FALSE 1431→ ] 1432→ sample_data(newphyseq) <- sample_data(new_DF) 1433→ newphyseq <- clean_pq(newphyseq) 1434→ if (is.null(taxonomic_rank) || type == "nb_seq") { 1435→ res[[f]] <- colnames(newphyseq@otu_table[, 1436→ colSums(newphyseq@otu_table) > min_nb_seq 1437→ ]) 1438→ } else { 1439→ res[[f]] <- 1440→ as.character(stats::na.exclude(unique(newphyseq@tax_table[ 1441→ colSums(newphyseq@otu_table) > min_nb_seq, 1442→ taxonomic_rank 1443→ ]))) 1444→ } 1445→ nb_seq <- 1446→ c( 1447→ nb_seq, 1448→ sum(physeq@otu_table[physeq@sam_data[[fact]] == f, ], na.rm = TRUE) 1449→ ) 1450→ 1451→ if (type == "nb_seq") { 1452→ res[[f]] <- unlist(sapply(res[[f]], function(x) { 1453→ paste0(x, "_", seq(1, taxa_sums(physeq)[[x]])) 1454→ })) 1455→ } 1456→ } 1457→ 1458→ if (max(nb_seq) / min(nb_seq) > 2 && verbose) { 1459→ message( 1460→ paste0( 1461→ "Two modalities differ greatly (more than x2) in their number of sequences (", 1462→ max(nb_seq), 1463→ " vs ", 1464→ min(nb_seq), 1465→ "). You may be interested by the parameter rarefy_after_merging" 1466→ ) 1467→ ) 1468→ } 1469→ 1470→ if (add_nb_samples) { 1471→ names(res) <- paste0(names(res), "\n (", nb_samples, " sam.)") 1472→ } 1473→ 1474→ if (add_nb_seq) { 1475→ names(res) <- paste0(names(res), "\n (", nb_seq, " seq.)") 1476→ } 1477→ 1478→ if (is.null(split_by)) { 1479→ p <- ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(res, ...) 1480→ } else { 1481→ modalities <- 1482→ as.factor(unlist(unclass(physeq@sam_data[[split_by]]))) 1483→ p <- vector("list", nlevels(modalities)) 1484→ names(p) <- levels(modalities) 1485→ for (moda in levels(modalities)) { 1486→ physeq_interm <- 1487→ clean_pq(subset_samples_pq(physeq, modalities == moda), silent = TRUE) 1488→ p[[moda]] <- ggvenn_pq( 1489→ physeq_interm, 1490→ fact = fact, 1491→ min_nb_seq = 0, 1492→ taxonomic_rank = NULL 1493→ ) + 1494→ ggtitle(moda) 1495→ } 1496→ } 1497→ if (return_data_for_venn) { 1498→ return(ggVennDiagram::process_data(ggVennDiagram::Venn(res))) 1499→ } else { 1500→ return(p) 1501→ } 1502→} 1503→################################################################################ 1504→ 1505→################################################################################ 1506→#' Multiple plot function 1507→#' 1508→#' @description 1509→#' 1510→#' 1511→#' 1512→#' 1513→#' ggplot objects can be passed in ..., or to plotlist (as a list of ggplot 1514→#' objects) 1515→#' 1516→#' If the layout is something like matrix(c(1,2,3,3), nrow=2, byrow=TRUE), 1517→#' then plot 1 will go in the upper left, 2 will go in the upper right, and 1518→#' 3 will go all the way across the bottom. 1519→#' 1520→#' @param ... list of ggplot objects 1521→#' @param plotlist list of ggplot objects 1522→#' @param cols number of columns 1523→#' @param layout A matrix specifying the layout. 1524→#' If present, 'cols' is ignored. 1525→#' @return Nothing. Print the list of ggplot objects 1526→#' @export 1527→ 1528→multiplot <- 1529→ function(..., plotlist = NULL, cols = 1, layout = NULL) { 1530→ # Make a list from the ... arguments and plotlist 1531→ plots <- c(list(...), plotlist) 1532→ 1533→ num_plots <- length(plots) 1534→ 1535→ # If layout is NULL, then use 'cols' to determine layout 1536→ if (is.null(layout)) { 1537→ # Make the panel 1538→ # ncol: Number of columns of plots 1539→ # nrow: Number of rows needed, calculated from # of cols 1540→ layout <- matrix( 1541→ seq(1, cols * ceiling(num_plots / cols)), 1542→ ncol = cols, 1543→ nrow = ceiling(num_plots / cols) 1544→ ) 1545→ } 1546→ 1547→ if (num_plots == 1) { 1548→ message(plots[[1]]) 1549→ } else { 1550→ # Set up the page 1551→ grid::grid.newpage() 1552→ grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport( 1553→ layout = grid::grid.layout( 1554→ nrow(layout), 1555→ ncol(layout) 1556→ ) 1557→ )) 1558→ 1559→ # Make each plot, in the correct location 1560→ for (i in seq(1, num_plots)) { 1561→ # Get the i,j matrix positions of the regions that contain this subplot 1562→ matchidx <- 1563→ as.data.frame(which(layout == i, arr.ind = TRUE)) 1564→ 1565→ print( 1566→ plots[[i]], 1567→ vp = grid::viewport( 1568→ layout.pos.row = matchidx$row, 1569→ layout.pos.col = matchidx$col 1570→ ) 1571→ ) 1572→ } 1573→ }

content.content: 1684→hill_pq <- function( 1685→ physeq, 1686→ fact = NULL, 1687→ variable = NULL, 1688→ hill_scales = c(0, 1, 2), 1689→ color_fac = NA, 1690→ letters = FALSE, 1691→ add_points = FALSE, 1692→ add_info = TRUE, 1693→ kruskal_test = TRUE, 1694→ one_plot = FALSE, 1695→ plot_with_tuckey = TRUE, 1696→ correction_for_sample_size = TRUE, 1697→ na_remove = TRUE, 1698→ vioplot = FALSE 1699→) { 1700→ if (!is.null(variable)) { 1701→ if (!is.null(fact)) { 1702→ stop( 1703→ "You must set only one parameter of variable or fact. This 2 1704→ parameters are strictly equivalent." 1705→ ) 1706→ } else { 1707→ variable_fac <- variable 1708→ } 1709→ } else { 1710→ if (!is.null(fact)) { 1711→ variable_fac <- fact 1712→ } else { 1713→ stop("You must set the parameter fact.") 1714→ } 1715→ } 1716→ var <- sym(variable_fac) 1717→ if (is.na(color_fac)) { 1718→ color_fac <- sym(variable_fac) 1719→ } else { 1720→ color_fac <- sym(color_fac) 1721→ } 1722→ 1723→ physeq <- taxa_as_rows(physeq) 1724→ if (na_remove || letters) { 1725→ physeq <- subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) 1726→ } 1727→ physeq@sam_data[[fact]] <- as.factor(physeq@sam_data[[fact]]) 1728→ 1729→ otu_hill <- 1730→ vegan::renyi(t(physeq)@otu_table, scales = hill_scales, hill = TRUE) 1731→ colnames(otu_hill) <- paste0("Hill_", hill_scales) 1732→ 1733→ df_hill <- data.frame(otu_hill, physeq@sam_data) 1734→ df_hill[, seq_along(hill_scales)] <- 1735→ apply(df_hill[, seq_along(hill_scales)], 2, as.numeric) 1736→ 1737→ p_var <- 1738→ hill_tuckey_pq( 1739→ physeq, 1740→ modality = variable_fac, 1741→ hill_scales = hill_scales, 1742→ correction_for_sample_size = correction_for_sample_size 1743→ ) 1744→ p_list <- vector("list", length(hill_scales)) 1745→ 1746→ if (kruskal_test) { 1747→ kt_res <- vector("list", length(hill_scales)) 1748→ for (i in seq_along(hill_scales)) { 1749→ kt_res[[i]] <- kruskal.test( 1750→ df_hill[, paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])], 1751→ df_hill[, fact] 1752→ ) 1753→ } 1754→ if ( 1755→ sum(sapply(kt_res, function(x) { 1756→ x$p.value > 0.05 1757→ })) > 1758→ 0 1759→ ) { 1760→ message(paste0( 1761→ sum(sapply(kt_res, function(x) { 1762→ x$p.value > 0.05 1763→ })), 1764→ " out of ", 1765→ length(kt_res), 1766→ " Hill scales do not show any global trends with you factor ", 1767→ fact, 1768→ ". Tuckey HSD plot is not informative for those Hill scales. Letters are not printed for those Hill scales" 1769→ )) 1770→ } 1771→ } 1772→ 1773→ for (i in seq_along(hill_scales)) { 1774→ if (vioplot) { 1775→ p_list[[i]] <- 1776→ ggplot( 1777→ df_hill, 1778→ aes( 1779→ x = .data[[paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])]], 1780→ y = !!var 1781→ ) 1782→ ) + 1783→ geom_violin(aes(colour = as.factor(!!color_fac))) + 1784→ labs(x = paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])) 1785→ } else { 1786→ p_list[[i]] <- 1787→ ggplot( 1788→ df_hill, 1789→ aes(group = !!var, x = .data[[paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])]]) 1790→ ) + 1791→ geom_boxplot( 1792→ outlier.size = 2, 1793→ aes(colour = as.factor(!!color_fac), y = !!var) 1794→ ) + 1795→ labs(x = paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])) 1796→ } 1797→ 1798→ if (add_points) { 1799→ p_list[[i]] <- 1800→ p_list[[i]] + 1801→ geom_jitter( 1802→ aes(y = !!var, colour = as.factor(!!color_fac)), 1803→ alpha = 0.5 1804→ ) 1805→ } 1806→ if (add_info) { 1807→ subtitle_plot <- paste0( 1808→ "Nb of samples: '", 1809→ paste0( 1810→ names(table(physeq@sam_data[[variable_fac]])), 1811→ sep = "' : ", 1812→ table(physeq@sam_data[[variable_fac]]), 1813→ collapse = " - '" 1814→ ) 1815→ ) 1816→ if (kruskal_test) { 1817→ subtitle_plot <- paste0( 1818→ subtitle_plot, 1819→ "\n", 1820→ paste0( 1821→ " Hill ", 1822→ hill_scales[[i]], 1823→ " -- Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared =", 1824→ round(kt_res[[i]]$statistic, 2), 1825→ "; df = ", 1826→ kt_res[[i]]$parameter, 1827→ "; p.value =", 1828→ format.pval(kt_res[[i]]$p.value, 2) 1829→ ) 1830→ ) 1831→ } 1832→ p_list[[i]] <- p_list[[i]] + labs(subtitle = subtitle_plot) 1833→ } 1834→ 1835→ if (letters) { 1836→ data_h <- 1837→ p_var$data[grep(paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]]), p_var$data[, 5]), ] 1838→ data_h_pval <- data_h$`p adj` 1839→ names(data_h_pval) <- data_h$modality 1840→ Letters <- 1841→ multcompView::multcompLetters(data_h_pval, reversed = TRUE)$Letters 1842→ 1843→ dt <- data.frame(variab = names(Letters), Letters = Letters) 1844→ names(dt) <- c(var, "Letters") 1845→ data_letters <- p_list[[i]]$data %>% 1846→ group_by(!!var) %>% 1847→ summarize( 1848→ pos_letters = max(.data[[paste0("Hill_", hill_scales[[i]])]]) + 1 1849→ ) %>% 1850→ inner_join(dt, by = join_by(!!fact)) 1851→ 1852→ if (!kruskal_test || kt_res[[i]]$p.value < 0.05) { 1853→ p_list[[i]] <- p_list[[i]] + 1854→ geom_label( 1855→ data = data_letters, 1856→ aes( 1857→ x = pos_letters, 1858→ label = Letters, 1859→ ), 1860→ y = unique(ggplot_build(p_list[[i]])$data[[1]]$y), 1861→ size = 4, 1862→ stat = "unique", 1863→ parse = TRUE 1864→ ) 1865→ } 1866→ } 1867→ } 1868→ 1869→ res <- p_list 1870→ names(res) <- paste0("plot_Hill_", hill_scales) 1871→ if (plot_with_tuckey) { 1872→ res[["tuckey"]] <- p_var 1873→ } 1874→ 1875→ if (one_plot) { 1876→ requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE) 1877→ if (letters || !plot_with_tuckey) { 1878→ res[["tuckey"]] <- NULL 1879→ } 1880→ res <- patchwork::wrap_plots(res) 1881→ } 1882→ return(res) 1883→} plots for between-subjects comparisons of Hill Number 1888→#' 1889→#' @description 1890→#' 1891→#' 1892→#' 1893→#' 1894→#' Note that contrary to [hill_pq()], this function does not take into 1895→#' account for difference in the number of sequences per samples/modalities. 1896→#' You may use rarefy_by_sample = TRUE if the mean number of sequences per 1897→#' samples differs among modalities. 1898→#' 1899→#' Basically a wrapper of function [ggstatsplot::ggbetweenstats()] for 1900→#' object of class phyloseq 1901→#' @inheritParams clean_pq 1902→#' @param fact (required) The variable to test. Must be present in 1903→#' the `sam_data` slot of the physeq object. 1904→#' @param one_plot (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, return a unique 1905→#' plot with the three plot inside using the patchwork package. 1906→#' @param rarefy_by_sample (logical, default FALSE) If TRUE, rarefy 1907→#' samples using [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()] function 1908→#' @param rngseed (Optional). A single integer value passed to 1909→#' [phyloseq::rarefy_even_depth()], which is used to fix a seed for 1910→#' reproducibly random number generation (in this case, reproducibly 1911→#' random subsampling). If set to FALSE, then no fiddling with the RNG seed 1912→#' is performed, and it is up to the user to appropriately call set.seed 1913→#' beforehand to achieve reproducible results. Default is FALSE. 1914→#' @param verbose (logical). If TRUE, print additional information. 1915→#' @param ... Additional arguments passed on to [ggstatsplot::ggbetweenstats()] function. 1916→ 1917→#' @return Either an unique ggplot2 object (if one_plot is TRUE) or 1918→#' a list of 3 ggplot2 plot: 1919→#' - plot_Hill_0 : the ggbetweenstats of Hill number 0 (= species richness) 1920→#' against the variable fact 1921→#' - plot_Hill_1 : the ggbetweenstats of Hill number 1 (= Shannon index) 1922→#' against the variable fact 1923→#' - plot_Hill_2 : the ggbetweenstats of Hill number 2 (= Simpson index) 1924→#' against the variable fact 1925→#' 1926→#' @export 1927→#' @examples 1928→#' \donttest{ 1929→#' if (requireNamespace("ggstatsplot")) { 1930→#' p <- ggbetween_pq(data_fungi, fact = "Time", p.adjust.method = "BH") 1931→#' p[[1]] 1932→#' ggbetween_pq(data_fungi, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE) 1933→#' ggbetween_pq(data_fungi, fact = "Height", one_plot = TRUE, rarefy_by_sample = TRUE)

2492→biplot_pq <- function(
  2493→  physeq,
  2494→  fact = NULL,
  2495→  merge_sample_by = NULL,
  2496→  rarefy_after_merging = FALSE,
  2497→  rngseed = FALSE,
  2498→  verbose = TRUE,
  2499→  inverse_side = FALSE,
  2500→  left_name = NULL,
  2501→  left_name_col = "#4B3E1E",
  2502→  left_fill = "#4B3E1E",
  2503→  left_col = "#f3f2d9",
  2504→  right_name = NULL,
  2505→  right_name_col = "#1d2949",
  2506→  right_fill = "#1d2949",
  2507→  right_col = "#1d2949",
  2508→  log10trans = TRUE,
  2509→  nudge_y = c(0.3, 0.3),
  2510→  geom_label = FALSE,
  2511→  text_size = 3,
  2512→  size_names = 5,
  2513→  y_names = NA,
  2514→  ylim_modif = c(1, 1),
  2515→  nb_samples_info = TRUE,
  2516→  plotly_version = FALSE,
  2517→  ...
  2518→) {
  2519→  if (!is.null(merge_sample_by)) {
  2520→    if (nb_samples_info) {
  2521→      modality_1_nb <- table(physeq@sam_data[, merge_sample_by])[1]
  2522→      modality_2_nb <- table(physeq@sam_data[, merge_sample_by])[2]
  2523→    }
  2524→    physeq <- merge_samples2(physeq, merge_sample_by)
  2525→    physeq <- clean_pq(physeq)
  2526→  }
  2527→
  2528→  if (nsamples(physeq) != 2) {
  2529→    stop(
  2530→      "biplot_pq needs only two samples in the
  2531→    physeq object or a valid merge_sample_by parameter"
  2532→    )
  2533→  }
  2534→
  2535→  if (rarefy_after_merging) {
  2536→    if (as(rngseed, "logical")) {
  2537→      set.seed(rngseed)
  2538→      if (verbose) {
  2539→        message(
  2540→          "`set.seed(",
  2541→          rngseed,
  2542→          ")` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling."
  2543→        )
  2544→        message("Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.")
  2545→        message(
  2546→          "Try `set.seed(",
  2547→          rngseed,
  2548→          "); .Random.seed` for the full vector",
  2549→          sep = ""
  2550→        )
  2551→        message("...")
  2552→      }
  2553→    } else if (verbose) {
  2554→      message(
  2555→        "You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded\n",
  2556→        " the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n",
  2557→        "See `?set.seed`\n"
  2558→      )
  2559→      message("...")
  2560→    }
  2561→    physeq <- clean_pq(rarefy_even_depth(physeq, rngseed = rngseed))
  2562→  }
  2563→
  2564→  if (
  2565→    sample_sums(physeq)[1] / sample_sums(physeq)[2] > 2 ||
  2566→      sample_sums(physeq)[2] / sample_sums(physeq)[1] > 2
  2567→  ) {
  2568→    message(
  2569→      paste0(
  2570→        "The two modalities differ greatly (more than x2) in their number of sequences (",
  2571→        sample_sums(physeq)[1],
  2572→        " vs ",
  2573→        sample_sums(physeq)[2],
  2574→        "). You may be interested by the parameter rarefy_after_merging"
  2575→      )
  2576→    )
  2577→  }
  2578→
  2579→  if (is.null(fact)) {
  2580→    if (is.null(left_name)) {
  2581→      left_name <- "A"
  2582→    }
  2583→    if (is.null(right_name)) {
  2584→      right_name <- "B"
  2585→    }
  2586→    modality <- factor(c(left_name, right_name))
  2587→  } else {
  2588→    modality <-
  2589→      as.factor(eval(parse(
  2590→        text = paste("physeq@sam_data$", fact, sep = "")
  2591→      )))
  2592→  }
  2593→
  2594→  if (inverse_side) {
  2595→    modality <- factor(modality, rev(levels(as.factor(modality))))
  2596→  }
  2597→
  2598→  if (is.null(left_name)) {
  2599→    left_name <- levels(modality)[1]
  2600→  }
  2601→  if (is.null(right_name)) {
  2602→    right_name <- levels(modality)[2]
  2603→  }
  2604→
  2605→  if (!is.null(merge_sample_by) && nb_samples_info) {
  2606→    left_name <- paste0(left_name, " (", modality_1_nb, " samples)")
  2607→    right_name <-
  2608→      paste0(right_name, " (", modality_2_nb, " samples)")
  2609→  }
  2610→
  2611→  physeq@sam_data$modality <- modality
  2612→
  2613→  mdf <- phyloseq::psmelt(physeq)
  2614→  mdf <- mdf[mdf$Abundance > 0, ]
  2615→  # mdf <- dplyr::rename(mdf, Abundance = Abundance)
  2616→
  2617→  if (length(ylim_modif) == 1) {
  2618→    ylim_modif <- c(ylim_modif, ylim_modif)
  2619→  }
  2620→
  2621→  if (length(y_names) == 1) {
  2622→    y_names <- c(y_names, y_names)
  2623→  }
  2624→
  2625→  if (length(nudge_y) == 1) {
  2626→    nudge_y <- c(nudge_y, nudge_y)
  2627→  }
  2628→  if (log10trans) {
  2629→    mdf$Ab <- log10(mdf$Abundance + 1)
  2630→  } else {
  2631→    mdf$Ab <- mdf$Abundance
  2632→    nudge_y <- mean(mdf$Abundance) * nudge_y
  2633→  }
  2634→
  2635→  mdf$Ab[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[1]] <-
  2636→    -mdf$Ab[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[1]]
  2637→  mdf$Proportion <- paste0(
  2638→    round(
  2639→      100 *
  2640→        mdf$Abundance /
  2641→        sum(mdf$Abundance[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[2]]),
  2642→      2
  2643→    ),
  2644→    "%"
  2645→  )
  2646→  mdf$Proportion[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[1]] <-
  2647→    paste0(
  2648→      round(
  2649→        100 *
  2650→          mdf$Abundance[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[1]] /
  2651→          sum(mdf$Abundance[mdf$modality == levels(modality)[1]]),
  2652→        2
  2653→      ),
  2654→      "%"
  2655→    )
  2656→
  2657→  p <- mdf %>%
  2658→    ggplot(
  2659→      aes(
  2660→        x = stats::reorder(OTU, Abundance),
  2661→        y = Ab,
  2662→        fill = modality,
  2663→        names = OTU,
  2664→        Ab = Abundance,
  2665→        Proportion = Proportion
  2666→      ),
  2667→      ...
  2668→    ) +
  2669→    geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.6) +
  2670→    annotate(
  2671→      "rect",
  2672→      xmin = "Samples",
  2673→      xmax = "Samples",
  2674→      ymin = -max(mdf$Ab),
  2675→      ymax = max(mdf$Ab)
  2676→    ) +
  2677→    annotate(
  2678→      geom = "text",
  2679→      label = right_name,
  2680→      x = "Samples",
  2681→      y = ifelse(is.na(y_names), max(mdf$Ab) / 2, y_names[2]),
  2682→      hjust = 0.5,
  2683→      vjust = 0.5,
  2684→      size = size_names,
  2685→      fontface = "bold",
  2686→      col = right_name_col
  2687→    ) +
  2688→    annotate(
  2689→      geom = "text",
  2690→      label = left_name,
  2691→      x = "Samples",
  2692→      y = ifelse(is.na(y_names), (min(mdf$Ab) / 2), -y_names[1]),
  2693→      hjust = 0.5,
  2694→      vjust = 0.5,
  2695→      size = size_names,
  2696→      fontface = "bold",
  2697→      col = left_name_col
  2698→    ) +
  2699→    geom_hline(aes(yintercept = 0)) +
  2700→    scale_x_discrete(
  2701→      limits = c(
  2702→        names(sort(
  2703→          tapply(mdf$Abundance, mdf$OTU, sum)
  2704→        )),
  2705→        "Samples"
  2706→      )
  2707→    ) +
  2708→    ylim(min(mdf$Ab), max(mdf$Ab) * 1.1)
  2709→
  2710→  if (geom_label) {
  2711→    p <- p +
  2712→      geom_label(
  2713→        aes(
  2714→          label = Abundance,
  2715→          color = modality,
  2716→          fill = modality,
  2717→          alpha = 0.5,
  2718→          y = ifelse(Ab > 0, Ab + nudge_y[2], Ab + nudge_y[1])
  2719→        ),
  2720→        size = text_size
  2721→      )
  2722→  } else {
  2723→    p <- p +
  2724→      geom_text(
  2725→        aes(
  2726→          label = Abundance,
  2727→          color = modality,
  2728→          y = ifelse(Ab > 0, Ab + nudge_y[2], Ab + nudge_y[1])
  2729→        ),
  2730→        size = text_size
  2731→      )
  2732→  }
  2733→
  2734→  p <- p +
  2735→    coord_flip() +
  2736→    theme_minimal() +
  2737→    theme(
  2738→      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
  2739→      axis.ticks = element_blank()
  2740→    ) +
  2741→    scale_fill_manual(values = c(left_fill, right_fill)) +

content.content: 3674→upset_pq <- function( 3675→ physeq, 3676→ fact, 3677→ taxa_fill = NULL, 3678→ min_nb_seq = 0, 3679→ na_remove = TRUE, 3680→ numeric_fonction = sum, 3681→ rarefy_after_merging = FALSE, 3682→ rngseed = FALSE, 3683→ verbose = TRUE, 3684→ ... 3685→) { 3686→ if (!is.null(min_nb_seq)) { 3687→ physeq@otu_table[physeq@otu_table < min_nb_seq] <- 0 3688→ } 3689→ 3690→ if (na_remove) { 3691→ physeq <- 3692→ subset_samples_pq(physeq, !is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])) 3693→ } else { 3694→ physeq@sam_data[[fact]][is.na(physeq@sam_data[[fact]])] <- 3695→ "NA" 3696→ } 3697→ 3698→ physeq <- merge_samples2(physeq, fact) 3699→ 3700→ if (rarefy_after_merging) { 3701→ if (as(rngseed, "logical")) { 3702→ set.seed(rngseed) 3703→ if (verbose) { 3704→ message( 3705→ "`set.seed(", 3706→ rngseed, 3707→ ")` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling." 3708→ ) 3709→ message("Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.") 3710→ message( 3711→ "Try `set.seed(", 3712→ rngseed, 3713→ "); .Random.seed` for the full vector", 3714→ sep = "" 3715→ ) 3716→ message("...") 3717→ } 3718→ } else if (verbose) { 3719→ message( 3720→ "You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded\n", 3721→ " the random seed of your session for reproducibility.\n", 3722→ "See `?set.seed`\n" 3723→ ) 3724→ message("...") 3725→ } 3726→ physeq <- clean_pq(rarefy_even_depth(physeq, rngseed = rngseed)) 3727→ } 3728→ 3729→ psm <- psmelt(physeq) 3730→ samp_names <- unique(psm$Sample) 3731→ psm <- 3732→ psm %>% 3733→ mutate(val = TRUE) %>% 3734→ tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Sample, values_from = val) 3735→ psm[samp_names][is.na(psm[samp_names])] <- FALSE 3736→ 3737→ psm <- psm %>% filter(Abundance != 0) 3738→ psm[[fact]] <- as.character(psm[[fact]]) 3739→ 3740→ psm2 <- data.frame(lapply(psm, function(col) { 3741→ tapply(col, paste0(psm$OTU), function(vec) { 3742→ diff_fct_diff_class( 3743→ vec, 3744→ numeric_fonction = numeric_fonction, 3745→ na.rm = TRUE 3746→ ) 3747→ }) 3748→ })) %>% 3749→ arrange(., desc(Abundance)) 3750→ 3751→ colnames(psm2) <- colnames(psm) 3752→ 3753→ if (is.null(taxa_fill)) { 3754→ p <- 3755→ ComplexUpset::upset(psm2, intersect = samp_names, ...) + xlab(fact) 3756→ } else { 3757→ p <- ComplexUpset::upset( 3758→ psm2, 3759→ intersect = samp_names, 3760→ base_annotations = list(), 3761→ annotations = list( 3762→ "Taxa" = (ggplot(mapping = aes(fill = .data[[taxa_fill]])) + 3763→ geom_bar() + 3764→ ylab("Taxa per Class") + 3765→ theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.2, "cm")) + 3766→ theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12))) 3767→ ), 3768→ ... 3769→ ) + 3770→ xlab(fact) 3771→ } 3772→ 3773→ return(p) 3774→} 3775→################################################################################ 3776→ 3777→################################################################################ 3778→#' Test for differences between intersections 3779→#' 3780→#' @description 3781→#' 3782→#' 3783→#' 3784→#' See [upset_pq()] to plot upset. There is a bug with ggplot2 >= 4.0.0. Additional arguments passed on to the [ComplexUpset::upset_test()] 3791→#' 3792→#' @return A \code{\link[ggplot2]{ggplot}}2 plot 3793→#' @export 3794→#' @author Adrien Taudière 3795→#' 3796→#' @seealso [upset_pq()] 3797→#' @examples 3798→#' data(data_fungi) 3799→#' if (requireNamespace("ComplexUpset")) { 3800→#' upset_test_pq(data_fungi, "Height", var_to_test = c("OTU", "Class", "Guild")) 3801→#' upset_test_pq(data_fungi, "Time") 3802→#' } 3803→upset_test_pq <- 3804→ function( 3805→ physeq, 3806→ fact, 3807→ var_to_test = "OTU", 3808→ min_nb_seq = 0, 3809→ na_remove = TRUE, 3810→ 350→#' @param reorder Logical specifying whether to reorder the new (merged)
   351→#'   samples by name
   352→#' @param default_fun Default functions if funs is not set. Per default
   353→#'   the function unique_or_na is used. See `diff_fct_diff_class()` for
   354→#'   a useful alternative.
   355→#' @export
   356→#' @return A new phyloseq-class, otu_table or sam_data object depending on
   357→#'   the class of the x param
   358→#' @examples
   359→#' data(enterotype)
   360→#'
   361→#' # Merge samples with the same project and clinical status
   362→#' ps <- enterotype
   363→#' sample_data(ps) <- sample_data(ps) %>%
   364→#'   transform(Project.ClinicalStatus = Project:ClinicalStatus)
   365→#' sample_data(ps) %>% head()
   366→#' ps0 <- merge_samples2(ps, "Project.ClinicalStatus",
   367→#'   fun_otu = mean,
   368→#'   funs = list(Age = mean)
   369→#' )
   370→#' sample_data(ps0) %>% head()
   371→#' @author Michael R. McLaren (orcid: [0000-0003-1575-473X](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1575-473X)) modified by Adrien Taudiere
   372→setGeneric(
   373→  "merge_samples2",
   374→  function(x,
   375→           group,
   376→           fun_otu = sum,
   377→           funs = list(),
   378→           reorder = FALSE,
   379→           default_fun = unique_or_na) {
   380→    standardGeneric("merge_samples2")
   381→  }
   382→)
   383→
   384→#' @rdname merge_samples2
   385→setMethod(
   386→  "merge_samples2",
   387→  signature("phyloseq"),
   388→  function(x, group, fun_otu = sum, funs = list(), reorder = FALSE,
   389→           default_fun = unique_or_na) {
   390→    if (length(group) == 1) {
   391→      stopifnot(group %in% sample_variables(x))
   392→      group <- sample_data(x)[[group]]
   393→    } else {
   394→      stopifnot(identical(length(group), nsamples(x)))
   395→    }
   396→    # Drop samples with `is.na(group)`
   397→    if (anyNA(group)) {
   398→      warning("`group` has missing values; corresponding samples will be dropped")
   399→      x <- prune_samples(!is.na(group), x)
   400→      group <- group[!is.na(group)]
   401→    }
   402→    # Merge
   403→    otu.merged <- merge_samples2(otu_table(x), group,
   404→      fun_otu = fun_otu,
   405→      reorder = reorder
   406→    )
   407→    if (!is.null(access(x, "sam_data"))) {
   408→      sam.merged <- merge_samples2(sample_data(x), group, funs = funs, default_fun = default_fun)
   409→    } else {
   410→      sam.merged <- NULL
   411→    }
   412→    phyloseq(
   413→      otu.merged,
   414→      sam.merged,
   415→      access(x, "tax_table"),
   416→      access(x, "phy_tree"),
   417→      access(x, "refseq")
   418→    )
   419→  }
   420→)
   421→
   422→#' @rdname merge_samples2
   423→setMethod(
   424→  "merge_samples2",
   425→  signature("otu_table"),
   426→  function(x, group, fun_otu = sum, reorder = FALSE,
   427→           default_fun = unique_or_na) {
   428→    stopifnot(identical(length(group), nsamples(x)))
   429→    # Work with samples as rows, and remember to flip back at end if needed
   430→    needs_flip <- taxa_are_rows(x)
   431→    if (needs_flip) {
   432→      x <- t(x)
   433→    }
   434→    # Drop samples with `is.na(group)`
   435→    if (anyNA(group)) {
   436→      warning("`group` has missing values; corresponding samples will be dropped")
   437→      x <- x[!is.na(group), ]
   438→      group <- group[!is.na(group)]
   439→    }
   440→    # Merging; result is a matrix with taxa as columns and rownames
   441→    # corresponding to `group`
   442→    if (identical(fun_otu, sum)) {
   443→      x.merged <- rowsum(x, group, reorder = reorder)
   444→    } else {
   445→      stopifnot(!".group" %in% colnames(x))
   446→      x.merged <- x %>%
   447→        as("matrix") %>%
   448→        tibble::as_tibble(.name_repair = c("minimal")) %>%
   449→        cbind(.group = group) %>%
   450→        group_by(.group) %>%
   451→        summarise(across(everything(), purrr::as_mapper(fun_otu)))
   452→
   453→
   454→      if (reorder) {
   455→        x.merged <- x.merged %>% arrange(.group)
   456→      }
   457→      x.merged <- x.merged %>%
   458→        tibble::column_to_rownames(".group")
   459→    }
   460→    # Return an otu_table in the proper orientation
   461→    x.merged <- x.merged %>% otu_table(taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
   462→    if (needs_flip) {
   463→      x.merged <- t(x.merged)
   464→    }
   465→    return(x.merged)
   466→  }
   467→)
   468→
   469→#' @rdname merge_samples2
   470→setMethod(
   471→  "merge_samples2",
   472→  signature("sample_data"),
   473→  function(x, group, funs = list(), reorder = FALSE,
   474→           default_fun = unique_or_na) {
   475→    if (length(group) == 1) {
   476→      stopifnot(group %in% sample_variables(x))
   477→      group <- x[[group]]
   478→    } else {
   479→      stopifnot(identical(length(group), nsamples(x)))
   480→    }
   481→    # Drop samples with `is.na(group)`
   482→    if (anyNA(group)) {
   483→      warning("`group` has missing values; corresponding samples will be dropped")
   484→      x <- x[!is.na(group), ]
   485→      group <- group[!is.na(group)]
   486→    }
   487→    ## Set the functions f used to merge each sample variable.
   488→    # Named logical vector indicating whether each variable is in the funs
   489→    var_in_funs <- names(x) %>%
   490→      rlang::set_names(. %in% names(funs), .)
   491→    # For vars in the funs, run f through as_mapper; else, use the default f
   492→    funs <- purrr::map2(
   493→      var_in_funs, names(var_in_funs),
   494→      ~ if (.x) purrr::as_mapper(funs[[.y]]) else default_fun
   495→    )
   496→    ## Merge variable values, creating a new sample_data object with one row
   497→    ## per group.
   498→    # A "sample_data" object is a list of data variables (columns); strategy is
   499→    # to reduce each variable with `merge_groups()`, and then recombine into a
   500→    # data.frame. The call to `merge_groups()` will sort by `group` values,
   501→    # which we need to account for when setting the new sample names.
   502→    new_sample_names <- group %>%
   503→      unique() %>%
   504→      sort() %>%
   505→      as.character()
   506→    x.merged <- purrr::map2(
   507→      x, funs,
   508→      ~ merge_groups(.x, group = group, f = .y)
   509→    ) %>%
   510→      data.frame() %>%
   511→      vctrs::vec_set_names(new_sample_names)
   512→    ## Put back in initial order
   513→    if (!reorder) {
   514→      initial_order <- group %>%
   515→        unique() %>%
   516→        as.character()
   517→      x.merged <- x.merged[initial_order, , drop = FALSE]
   518→    }
   519→    ## Return as sample data with group names preserved
   520→    x.merged %>% MiscMetabar:::sample_data_stable()
   521→  }
   522→)
   523→
   524→
   525→# Helpers ---------------------------------------------------------------------
   526→
   527→#' Get the unique value in x or NA if none
   528→#'
   529→#' If `unique(x)` is a single value, return it; otherwise, return an NA of the
   530→#' same type as `x`. If `x` is a factor, then the levels and ordered status
   531→#' will be kept in either case. If `x` is a non-atomic vector (i.e. a list),
   532→#' then the logical `NA` will be used.
   533→#'
   534→#' @param x A vector
   535→#' @export
   536→#' @return Either a single value (if `unique(x)` return a single value) or a NA
   537→#' @author Michael R. McLaren (orcid: [0000-0003-1575-473X](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1575-473X))
   538→#' @examples
   539→#' f <- factor(c("a", "a", "b", "c"), ordered = TRUE)
   540→#' unique_or_na(f)
   541→#' unique_or_na(f[1:2])
   542→#'
   543→#' x <- c("a", "b", "a")
   544→#' unique_or_na(x[c(1, 3)])
   545→#' unique_or_na(x)
   546→#' unique_or_na(x) %>% typeof()
   547→unique_or_na <- function(x) {
   548→  UseMethod("unique_or_na")
   549→}

109→accu_plot <-
   110→  function(
   111→    physeq,
   112→    fact = NULL,
   113→    add_nb_seq = TRUE,
   114→    step = NULL,
   115→    by.fact = FALSE,
   116→    ci_col = NULL,
   117→    col = NULL,
   118→    lwd = 3,
   119→    leg = TRUE,
   120→    print_sam_names = FALSE,
   121→    ci = 2,
   122→    ...
   123→  ) {
   124→    if (!inherits(physeq, "phyloseq")) {
   125→      stop("physeq must be a phyloseq object")
   126→    }
   127→
   128→    if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq)) {
   129→      physeq@otu_table <-
   130→        otu_table(t(physeq@otu_table), taxa_are_rows = TRUE)
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    if (!add_nb_seq) {
   134→      factor_interm <-
   135→        eval(parse(text = paste("physeq@sam_data$", fact, sep = "")))
   136→      factor_interm <- as.factor(factor_interm)
   137→
   138→      physeq_accu <- as.matrix(t(physeq@otu_table))
   139→      physeq_accu[physeq_accu > 0] <- 1
   140→      accu_all <- vegan::specaccum(physeq_accu)
   141→
   142→      accu <- vector("list", nlevels(factor_interm))
   143→      for (i in seq_along(levels(factor_interm))) {
   144→        accu[[i]] <-
   145→          vegan::specaccum(physeq_accu[

   147→          ])

   149→

   151→        col <- funky_color(nlevels(factor_interm) + 1)
   152→      }
   153→      if (is.null(ci_col)) {
   154→        transp <- function(col, alpha = 0.5) {
   155→          res <-
   156→            apply(grDevices::col2rgb(col), 2, function(c) {
   157→              grDevices::rgb(c[1] / 255, c[2] / 255, c[3] / 255, alpha)
   158→            })
   159→          return(res)
   160→        }
   161→        ci_col <-
   162→          transp(funky_color(nlevels(factor_interm) + 1), 0.3)
   163→      }
   164→
   165→      plot(
   166→        accu_all,
   167→        # ci_type = "poly",
   168→        # ci_col = ci_col[1],
   169→        col = col[1],
   170→        lwd = lwd,
   171→        # ci_lty = 0,
   172→        xlab = "Sample",
   173→        ...
   174→      )
   175→
   176→      for (i in seq_along(levels(factor_interm))) {
   177→        graphics::lines(accu[[i]], col = col[i + 1], lwd = lwd)
   178→      }
   179→      if (leg) {
   180→        graphics::legend(
   181→          "bottomright",
   182→          c("all", levels(factor_interm)),
   183→          col = col,
   184→          lty = 1,
   185→          lwd = 3
   186→        )
   187→      }
   188→    }
   189→
   190→    if (add_nb_seq) {
   191→      fact_interm <-
   192→        as.factor(unlist(unclass(physeq@sam_data[, fact])[fact]))
